title: get() unit tests using mock kube HTTP layer
description:
What: Write unit tests for K8sBackend::get() using the kube mock HTTP layer (tower::ServiceExt / kube::client::mock) so no real Kubernetes cluster is required.

Algorithm:
1. Add to Cargo.toml dev-dependencies: tower = {version="0.4", features=["util"]}, http = "1", serde_json = {workspace=true}.
2. Use kube::Client::new(mock_service, default_namespace) to construct a test client:
   - kube provides a way to inject a custom tower::Service as the HTTP transport.
   - Pattern: kube::Client::new(tower::service_fn(|req| async { ... }), "default")
3. For each test, construct a K8sBackend with a pre-initialized client cell, bypassing from_uri's lazy init.
   - Use a test helper: fn backend_with_client(ns, name, key, client) -> K8sBackend
4. Implement test cases:
   a. test_get_single_key_no_key_param: mock returns Secret with data={"value": ByteString(b"hello")}; no ?key= -> value bytes == b"hello". Oracle: b"hello" is the known input.
   b. test_get_with_key_param: mock returns Secret with data={"password":b"secret123","user":b"admin"}; ?key=password -> b"secret123". Oracle: b"secret123".
   c. test_get_multi_key_no_key_param: mock returns Secret with 2 keys; no ?key= -> Err(InvalidUri). Assert matches!(r, Err(SecretError::InvalidUri(_))).
   d. test_get_not_found: mock returns HTTP 404 -> Err(NotFound). Assert matches!(r, Err(SecretError::NotFound)).
   e. test_get_empty_data: mock returns Secret with data=None -> Err(NotFound).
   f. test_get_key_absent: mock returns Secret with data={"other":b"x"}; ?key=missing -> Err(NotFound).
5. All assertions: assert_eq!(result.unwrap().as_bytes(), b"expected") — never print the value.

Defensive programming rules:
- Test assertions must compare as_bytes() against a &[u8] literal; never print or log.
- Each test must check the specific error variant, not just is_err().
- Mock service must not make real HTTP calls.
- Test helper that constructs K8sBackend with injected client must NOT be pub (test-only).

Subagent instructions: Spawn research subagent to find kube mock HTTP examples in kube crate tests (look in ~/.cargo/registry/src/*/kube-*/tests/ or kube-client/src/). Spawn validation subagent to run cargo test -p secretx-k8s after writing.

Tests listed above in Algorithm step 4.
